mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a strong and stable labor market, which supports robust consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE consistently below 4% is associated with negative 20d SPX returns in more than 50% of observations over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) reaches a high level, it signals elevated inflation, which typically prompts central bank tightening, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL values above 290 are consistently followed by positive 60d SPX returns in more than 40% of triggered instances over a 7-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) combined with strong industrial production (INDPRO) indicates broad economic strength, high consumer confidence, and healthy manufacturing activity, collectively driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If periods where both RSAFS and INDPRO show positive month-over-month growth are not associated with positive 20d SPX returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained monetary tightening, indicated by a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS), higher interest rates increase the cost of capital and reduce the present value of future earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If during identified monetary tightening cycles, the S&P 500 consistently generates positive 60d returns following FEDFUNDS increases in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts (HOUST) above a key threshold signals robust construction activity and strong consumer confidence in the housing market, which typically precedes broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If HOUST values above 1500 are followed by negative 5d SPX returns in more than 55% of occurrences over any 10-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signifies a robust and expanding job market, leading to increased consumer spending and corporate revenues, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If periods of positive PAYEMS month-over-month growth are associated with negative 20d SPX returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarter-over-quarter annualized growth in real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) reflects overall economic expansion and increased productivity, which directly supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth above 2.5% is consistently followed by negative 60d SPX returns in more than 40% of instances over any 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) and low unemployment (UNRATE) signals an overheating economy, which typically forces the central bank to aggressively tighten monetary policy, negatively impacting equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If periods where CPIAUCSL is above 290 and UNRATE is below 4.0 are consistently followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 45% of observations.
